meta_title: For Students: Antigravity project template to Solve can't debug code (Intermediate Tutorial)
meta_description: Learn how to Antigravity project template and overcome can't debug code. A Intermediate-level Tutorial using Antigravity and Claude Code.

content.seo_title: Antigravity Project Template: Solve Debugging Hurdles Now
content.seo_description: Master the Antigravity project template to eliminate debugging roadblocks. A professional, intermediate-level tutorial for students using Claude Code.
content.answer_block: Stop staring at cryptic stack traces. By implementing the Antigravity project template, you standardize your codebase architecture, enabling automated diagnostic loops. When paired with Claude Code, you transform manual debugging into an agentic workflow that identifies, isolates, and patches logical errors in real-time, drastically reducing your development cycle time.
content.intro:
You’re staring at the screen at 2 AM. The console is a sea of red text, and your application—once a beacon of potential—is now a tangled web of unidentifiable bugs. We’ve all been there; that moment where your ambition hits the wall of technical debt and you wonder if you’re cut out for this.

But here is the secret: senior engineers don't just 'code better'; they build better environments. By adopting the Antigravity project template, you move away from chaotic, manual debugging and into a structured, agent-assisted workflow. This isn't just about fixing a bug; it's about upgrading your entire development infrastructure to handle complexity with professional-grade precision.

steps.title: Initialize the Antigravity Scaffold
steps.description: Standardize your project structure to ensure all modules are injectable and observable for AI agents.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Scaffold a new project using the Antigravity template, ensuring strict adherence to modular architecture and observability patterns.

steps.title: Inject Observability Hooks
steps.description: Add tracing to your core functions so the AI can 'see' the state of variables during runtime.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Analyze my current codebase and inject Antigravity observability hooks into all asynchronous functions to track execution flow.

steps.title: Automate Error Pattern Mapping
steps.description: Create a map of common failure states within your project so the AI can identify patterns faster.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Create an error-manifest.json file in the root directory that maps common runtime exceptions to potential root causes in this stack.

steps.title: Execute Agentic Diagnostic Loop
steps.description: Trigger Claude Code to run a deep analysis on specific failing modules using the Antigravity context.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Run a diagnostic sweep on the auth-module using the existing error-manifest and observability data.

steps.title: Apply Validated Fixes
steps.description: Review and apply the AI-suggested patches to your codebase while maintaining system integrity.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Review the diagnostic findings and apply the necessary patches, ensuring no regression in existing unit tests.

pro_tips: Always maintain a clean 'error-manifest.json'—it serves as the long-term memory for your AI agent.
Use 'dry-run' mode in Claude Code before applying patches to see the diffs in a controlled environment.
Tag your functions with metadata decorators; this drastically improves the agent's contextual awareness.
Integrate your CI/CD pipeline with Antigravity logs to catch bugs before they reach production.
Treat your debug logs as documentation; clear logs make the code 10x easier for AI to interpret.
content.next_steps: Once your first project is stable, focus on building a custom 'Agentic Library' for your recurring project types to automate setup even further.
content.conversion_block: The difference between a student and a senior dev is the toolkit they command. Stop fighting your code and start architecting your success with the right scaffolding. Ready to build? Join our AI Builder community → Start your first project today.
